mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment above a critical threshold signals weak aggregate demand and depressed corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market slack persists into 2011
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 8.0% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate, maintained by the central bank, reduces the discount rate for future earnings and encourages risk-taking behavior, boosting equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC statements on accommodative stance post-2008
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns becomes positive and significant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in retail sales indicates strengthening consumer demand, which translates directly to higher expected corporate revenues and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is the largest component of US GDP
failure_condition: A positive 3-month moving average of retail sales growth is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is accelerating while inflation remains contained, it indicates a healthy, non-inflationary expansion. This 'goldilocks' scenario is highly favorable for corporate profit margins and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year INDPRO growth > 3% AND Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth < 4%
evidence_refs: fact:Historical periods of non-inflationary growth have been bullish for equities
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to generate positive median 20-day returns during periods where the trigger condition is met for at least 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate environment, strong nonfarm payrolls growth is not perceived as a threat that will induce monetary tightening. Instead, it is viewed purely as a sign of strengthening aggregate demand, leading to a strong positive market reaction.
evidence_refs: fact:Market reactions to jobs reports differ based on the monetary policy regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, instances of PAYEMS growth > 200k are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sustained drop in housing starts below a key cyclical level indicates a severe contraction in a critical, interest-rate sensitive sector of the economy, portending a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2007-2009 housing collapse was a leading indicator of the GFC
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in over 50% of months where HOUST is below 600k.

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank holds rates near zero, an acceleration in inflation creates negative real interest rates and signals an unavoidable future tightening cycle, creating uncertainty and leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary environments are historically poor for equity returns
failure_condition: The S&P 500 shows a statistically significant positive 20-day forward return when the trigger condition is met over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in the industrial production index signals a contraction in the manufacturing and production sectors. This is a classic leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, leading to lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 10-year period.
